concept: THE FAKE THAT SWAM — an AI-generated image claimed a beluga escaped the Alaska SeaLife Center in Seward to find her calf; it drew close to six million views before the people who knew the whale set the record straight. The film stages the true whale, the viral fake, the moment the fabrication frays under a close look (where a detector can only put it at 82.6 percent likely AI, not proof), and the institution's plain correction, then loops back to the truth.

fingerprint.palette: aquarium teal-black water, pearl beluga white, a toxic synthetic magenta, phone-glow cyan, a warning-red correction stamp
fingerprint.metaphor: the fake beluga dissolves into generated pixels while the real one holds; truth is the thing the machine cannot certify

palette: aquarium teal-black water, pearl beluga white, a toxic synthetic magenta, phone-glow cyan, a warning-red correction stamp

light.sun: no sun; a night interior lit from within, the soft practical glow of an aquarium tank against the hard cold glare of a phone feed
light.mood: the real whale is lit gently from inside her water; the fake is lit by the flat blue light of a screen. Warm inner truth versus cold outer glare.

hook.frame1: A pearl-white beluga hangs in dark teal tank water, calm and real; the burned-in headline sits lower third over her, a warning-red hairline framing the frame like a feed about to lie.
hook.headline: AI FAKED THIS WHALE'S ESCAPE
hook.loopback: The film opens on the real beluga at rest in her tank and returns to that exact calm frame at the close, the truth still there after the fake has dissolved.

hero.name: the real beluga
hero.detail_note: rounded melon, long pale body, two pectoral fins, broad fluke, a dark eye; three read-zones head/body/tail; enters first as a soft SILHOUETTE against the lit tank glass (a distinct, non-portrait entrance) before the orbit reveals her fully; skin catches one soft specular from the tank light

shots.centered: the beluga surfaces from a silhouette against the glass to full relief as the camera orbits and her eye comes into focus
shots.means: establish the true subject in her real place before any lie touches it

hero.name: the phone feed
hero.detail_note: phone body, glowing screen holding the fake image, a view-counter, a bezel and notch; zones screen/counter/chrome; the counter spins toward six million

shots.centered: the view counter at frame center spins up toward 6,000,000 as the fake image sits behind it
shots.means: carry the whale's image out of the tank and onto the feed, where the number is the story

hero.name: the fabricated beluga
hero.detail_note: a beluga shape that is right at a glance but wrong up close: edges break into voxel cubes and magenta glitch, fins fray into pixels; zones intact-body/dissolving-edge

shots.centered: the fake whale fills frame center and, as we push in, sheds pixels radially; a DETECTOR readout reads 82.6% LIKELY AI, and likely is not proof
shots.means: the honest limit, drawn: the fake looks real and a detector can only give odds (82.6% likely), not proof; the tell shows only up close

hero.name: the correction card
hero.detail_note: a lit institutional fact-stamp assembling: 'SINCE 1998' rule, '0 ESCAPES' rule, and a red THIS NEVER HAPPENED stamp over the collapsing fake; zones fact-block/stamp

shots.centered: the correction stamp lands dead center as the fake collapses out of the grid behind it
shots.means: the people who knew the whale set the record straight; the fake falls, the fact stands

hero.name: the real beluga, restored
hero.detail_note: the same beluga from shot 1 back at rest in her true tank, calm; the brand wordmark resolves lower third; a loop-back to the opening frame

shots.centered: the real beluga settles at frame center, the wordmark resolving beneath her
shots.means: loop back to the truth that was there all along; the button lands on knowing, not on a better machine

beats.vo: This beluga never left her tank in Seward.
beats.shows: a beluga silhouette against lit tank glass resolves to a full pearl-white whale as she glides in
beats.sfx: soft sub-aqua swell + a low water-room tone

choreo.primary: the whale surfaces from a glass silhouette and traverses frame left to right on an eased path
choreo.reaction: caustic light ripples slide across her flank as she resolves out of silhouette
choreo.ambient: slow particulate drift in the water column

beats.vo: But a picture said she did.
beats.shows: a hairline red frame ticks in around the image, feed-style
beats.sfx: a single UI tick as the red frame snaps in
beats.means: a feed is about to speak for her

choreo.primary: red hairline frame draws in around the tank image
choreo.reaction: as the frame snaps in, the tank key-light dips a notch on her back (the feed's frame dimming the real light)
choreo.ambient: faint grain crawls over the water

beats.vo: It showed her slipping out to sea to find her calf.
beats.shows: a false open-sea plate wipes over the tank; a small calf-silhouette appears ahead and she angles toward it
beats.sfx: a distant open-water wash fading up under the room tone
beats.means: the lie's content, drawn: an escape to reunite with a calf

choreo.primary: a false open-ocean plate slides over the tank behind her as she drifts toward open water
choreo.reaction: a small second beluga (the calf) fades in ahead and she angles toward it
choreo.ambient: cold open-water rays replace the warm tank caustics

beats.vo: The image was fake, built by AI, and it looked completely real.
beats.shows: a phone rises into frame carrying the same whale image; fui-boot
beats.sfx: a whoosh as the phone rises + a soft screen-boot chime
beats.means: carry the image onto the feed

choreo.primary: phone rises into frame center and its screen boots
choreo.reaction: the tank behind defocuses as the screen takes over

beats.vo: One post drew close to six million views
beats.shows: the view counter mounts, digits rolling
beats.sfx: rapid odometer ticks climbing in pitch
beats.means: the number is the story now

choreo.reaction: a like/share row pops in stutters beside it
choreo.ambient: feed cards scroll faintly behind the counter

beats.shows: counter nears six million and holds; a small warning under it
beats.sfx: the ticks resolve to a held tone as the counter locks

choreo.primary: counter eases to a stop near 6,000,000
choreo.reaction: an ILLUSTRATIVE micro-label blinks under the number

beats.vo: A checker could only say it looked likely fake.
beats.shows: a DETECTOR strip scans and settles on 82.6% LIKELY AI
beats.sfx: a scanning pulse then a soft uncertain tone

choreo.primary: a scan line sweeps the fake image on screen
choreo.reaction: the DETECTOR readout settles on 82.6% LIKELY AI in amber

beats.vo: What stopped it was people who actually knew the whale.
beats.shows: morph into the fabricated beluga at macro; a handler's hand and the Center mark ghost at the glass
beats.sfx: a morph whoosh into a quieter, closer water tone
beats.means: the humans who knew her enter the frame, against the fabrication

choreo.primary: the screen image morphs into a full fabricated whale, convincing at a glance
choreo.reaction: a handler's silhouette and hand rise at the tank glass and the SeaLife Center mark ghosts in
choreo.ambient: slow drift of synthetic haze behind the glass

beats.vo: The Alaska SeaLife Center has run since 1998,
beats.shows: a SINCE 1998 fact-plate draws in over the fraying fake as its edges shed voxels
beats.sfx: a crackle riser as pixels start to peel
beats.means: the true record arrives as the lie frays

choreo.primary: camera dollies through; fake edges break into cubes
choreo.reaction: a SINCE 1998 rule draws in at lower third, in time with the VO

beats.vo: and no beluga has ever escaped.
beats.shows: a specific tell fails: the fake's eye blows into cubes and the fluke voxelizes; a 0 ESCAPES plate joins
beats.sfx: riser peaks then a deliberate dip toward silence
beats.means: the specific tell + the second fact, then the breath before the correction

choreo.primary: the eye pixelates and the fluke shears apart into voxels (a named tell, not a general blur)
choreo.reaction: a 0 ESCAPES rule snaps in beside SINCE 1998
choreo.ambient: the DETECTOR strip dims and pixel motes stream off the tail

beats.vo: So they said it plainly. This never happened.
beats.shows: hard cut; the SINCE 1998 and 0 ESCAPES plates lock into a clean grid, then a red stamp lands
beats.sfx: silence, then a firm stamp hit and settle boom

choreo.primary: the SINCE 1998 and 0 ESCAPES plates lock square into a clean grid
choreo.reaction: a red THIS NEVER HAPPENED stamp slams over the collapsing fake
choreo.ambient: the fake's last pixels sink out of the grid

beats.shows: the feed panel greys out and collapses
beats.sfx: a short power-down sweep as the panel dies

choreo.primary: the feed card desaturates and folds away
choreo.reaction: the view counter freezes then goes dark

beats.vo: AI can draw a lie faster than we can prove it false.
beats.shows: a fast red FAKE line races across; a slow grey PROOF bar crawls behind and never catches it
beats.means: the real limit, drawn as a race the proof loses

choreo.primary: a red 'fake' line shoots left to right at speed
choreo.reaction: a slow grey 'proof' bar inches after it and falls behind
choreo.ambient: residual pixel-motes clear off the right edge (a third, disjoint region)

beats.vo: The catch was not a smarter machine.
beats.shows: crossfade back to the real beluga at rest in her tank

choreo.primary: the real tank fades up, beluga at rest
choreo.reaction: caustics resume gently across her back

beats.vo: It was a place that knew the truth. Who is yours?
beats.shows: the beluga settles center; the ALASKA.AI wordmark resolves
beats.sfx: a soft resolving chime, fade to the signoff

choreo.primary: camera micro-drift holds on the calm whale

beats.shows: a last caustic sweep crosses her back as the tagline resolves and the loop-frame clicks
beats.sfx: a soft low shimmer under the tagline
beats.means: close the loop, no dead air on the tail

choreo.primary: a caustic light sweep travels across the whale's back
choreo.reaction: the tagline eases up under the wordmark
choreo.ambient: tank particulate drifts, matching the opening frame

honest_caveat: A detector could only put odds on the fake, about 82.6 percent likely AI by one tool (Snopes via Hive Moderation), and likely is not proof. The image had already drawn millions of views. What actually settled it was an Alaska institution that knew its own animals and said plainly it could not have happened. The machine can generate a lie and even estimate one, but it cannot certify the truth.
voice_plan: a calm, credible male narrator (edge-tts en-US-AndrewMultilingualNeural as the publish voice this run; kokoro is unavailable in this environment because its required g2p model download is blocked by network policy), riding the picture beat for beat
on_screen_numbers: ~6,000,000 VIEWS (illustrative; reported by Alaska Public Media)
divergence_note: Every recent Dispatch framed AI as a helpful instrument that detects, counts or predicts nature (salmon sonar, caribou, volcano tremor, smoke forecasts). This one inverts that: AI is the source of a convincing LIE, and the honest limit is that detection failed and human/institutional knowledge was the backstop. Visually it abandons the last two boards entirely: versus 2026-07-11 (orbital-aerial storm-descent over smoke, stacked strata, naturalistic landscape) it is an eye-level night interior in tank water orbiting a single organic hero; versus 2026-07-10 (macro vertical-descent split on a river) it swaps to a horizontal orbit-reveal with a night-practical palette of aquarium teal, pearl white, synthetic magenta and phone-glow cyan. The metaphor (a fabricated whale dissolving into generated pixels while the real one holds) is a new central image, and the camera strategy is orbit-reveal, not the previous aerial-descent.
